book_claims.claim: Navy beans (white/cannellini beans) are used in multiple Longevity Diet dishes including the Molochio pasta e vaianeia, Ligurian minestrone, and white bean salad, recommended at 150 g wet weight (drained) per serving.
book_claims.mechanism: Plant protein source associated with longevity in centenarian populations; fiber feeds beneficial gut bacteria; phytochemicals reduce chronic disease risk.

book_claims.claim: White beans are a notable iron source (8 mg per cup canned, 44% DV) and calcium source (81 mg per ½ cup cooked, 8% DV).
book_claims.mechanism: Plant-based iron supports oxygen transport; calcium contributes to bone mineral density maintenance in aging adults.

supplementary_research.source: Bazzano et al., Archives of Internal Medicine (2001) — PMID 11176744
supplementary_research.finding: In a prospective cohort of 9,632 US adults, legume consumption ≥4 times per week versus <1 time per week was associated with a 22% lower risk of coronary heart disease, firmly establishing legumes including navy beans as longevity foods.

White navy beans contain high levels of resistant starch and soluble fiber that reduce postprandial glucose response by up to 25%, improve insulin sensitivity, and promote satiety — metabolic benefits directly relevant to healthy aging and weight management.

supplementary_research.source: Mudryj et al., Applied Physiology, Nutrition, and Metabolism (2012) — PMID 22540923
supplementary_research.finding: Legume consumption is the single most consistent dietary predictor of longevity across Blue Zone populations in Sardinia, Okinawa, Loma Linda, and Nicoya, with bean intake associated with a 7–8% reduction in mortality risk per 20 g/day increment.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: PDCAAS ~0.68; complements grains to provide all essential amino acids; methionine-limiting.
Among the highest fiber contents of any legume; high in soluble fiber that reduces LDL cholesterol and feeds butyrate-producing bacteria.
Fermented by colonic bacteria to short-chain fatty acids (butyrate, propionate); reduces glycaemic index and supports colon health.
High natural folate content; important for DNA synthesis and homocysteine metabolism.

synergies.description: Pasta e fagioli is the archetypal Longevity Diet pairing; together they form a complete protein and provide sustained energy with low glycaemic impact.
Olive oil's oleocanthal reduces inflammation from lectins; fat improves absorption of fat-soluble vitamins in the dish.
Tomato vitamin C converts non-heme iron from beans to the more absorbable ferrous form, meaningfully improving iron uptake.
